section.heading: Termination of committees
section.chapeau: The Secretary shall terminate any advisory committee upon a finding that any such advisory committee—

paragraph.content: has expended funds in excess of its estimated annual operating costs by more than 10 per centum or $500, whichever is greater, without the prior approval of the Secretary pursuant to the provisions of section 2285 of this title;
has failed to file all reports required under the provisions of chapter 10 of title 5 or this chapter;
has failed to meet for two consecutive years;
is responsible for functions that otherwise would be or should be performed by Federal employees; or
does not serve or has ceased to serve an essential public function.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2022—Par. (2). Pub. L. 117–286 substituted “chapter 10 of title 5” for “the Federal Advisory Committee Act”.
1981—Pub. L. 97–98 substituted provision relating to termination of advisory committees for provision relating to advisory committee charter requirements.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1981 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 97–98 effective Dec. 22, 1981, see section 1801 of Pub. L. 97–98, set out as an Effective Date note under section 4301 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective Oct. 1, 1977, see section 1901 of Pub. L. 95–113, set out as an Effective Date of 1977 Amendment note under section 1307 of this title.
